source: CISA SCuBA — Microsoft 365 Entra ID Security Configuration Baseline
description: Policies derived from the CISA Secure Cloud Business Applications (SCuBA) Security Configuration Baseline for Microsoft 365 Entra ID (MS.AAD). Only controls that require a Conditional Access policy are included.

policies.name: Block Legacy Authentication
policies.fullName: MS.AAD.1.1 — Legacy authentication SHALL be blocked
policies.description: Legacy authentication protocols do not support modern authentication controls including MFA. All legacy authentication clients (Exchange ActiveSync and Other Clients) SHALL be blocked via Conditional Access.

policies.name: Block High Risk Users
policies.fullName: MS.AAD.2.1 — Users detected as high risk SHALL be blocked
policies.description: Users with a high Identity Protection user risk score SHALL be blocked from authentication via Conditional Access until the risk is remediated by an administrator.

policies.name: Block High Risk Sign-Ins
policies.fullName: MS.AAD.2.3 — Sign-ins detected as high risk SHALL be blocked
policies.description: Sign-ins with a high Identity Protection sign-in risk score SHALL be blocked or require MFA with password change via Conditional Access.

policies.name: Phishing-Resistant MFA for All Users
policies.fullName: MS.AAD.3.1 — Phishing-resistant MFA SHALL be enforced for all users
policies.description: All users SHALL be required to use phishing-resistant MFA (e.g. FIDO2, Windows Hello for Business, or certificate-based authentication) via a Conditional Access authentication strength policy.

policies.name: MFA for All Users
policies.fullName: MS.AAD.3.2 — MFA SHALL be enforced for all users
policies.description: All users SHALL be required to use multi-factor authentication via Conditional Access. This is the minimum requirement if phishing-resistant MFA (MS.AAD.3.1) has not yet been fully deployed.

policies.name: Phishing-Resistant MFA for Privileged Users
policies.fullName: MS.AAD.3.6 — Phishing-resistant MFA SHALL be required for users with highly privileged roles
policies.description: Users assigned to highly privileged roles (Global Administrator, Privileged Role Administrator, etc.) SHALL be required to use phishing-resistant MFA via a Conditional Access authentication strength policy.

policies.name: Managed Device Required for User Access
policies.fullName: MS.AAD.3.7 — Managed devices SHOULD be required for user access
policies.description: All users accessing cloud applications SHOULD be required to use a managed device (compliant or Entra hybrid joined) via Conditional Access.

policies.name: Managed Device Required for Security Info Registration
policies.fullName: MS.AAD.3.8 — Managed devices SHOULD be required for device registration and security info registration
policies.description: Users registering MFA methods (security information) SHOULD be required to do so from a managed device or a trusted location to prevent registration hijacking.
